toolUseResult.newString:
## APPROVED AND READY TO DISPATCH: #33 Playwright (owner ruling + amendment, 2026-07-23)
Owner approved in chat AND on the issue, for provenance. Prior-art question CLOSED: none
to port — the sibling is a C# program using the Avalonia MCP and on-rig testing, so its
UI-verification pattern does not cross to a browser view. **The owner's "amendment
approved" covers all three conductor refinements, which BIND the eventual brief:**
1. Playwright **library**, NOT `@playwright/test` — `board/web` runs bare `node --test`
   with no config; a second runner would mean two ways to write a test forever.
2. Screenshot **regeneration** only, **never pixel diffing** — CI has windows + ubuntu
   legs and their rasterisation differs, so a pixel assertion would cry wolf across legs
   (the 54-flags-50-false scar). Assert on `getComputedStyle`, not pixels.
3. Sequencing noted: #32 is a BROKEN existing instrument; #33 is a new one. A lying
   instrument poisons everything downstream, so #32 arguably goes first.
NIRTS scope: ONE spec inside the existing `node --test` suite that serves the real board
from the real store, reds first on the #31 defect (a nominal row inside a hot lane),
asserts computed colour per register, regenerates the screenshot. No second runner, no
pixel diffs, no page objects.
**Not dispatched yet — deliberately.** Cost discipline: one unit at a time while the
owner's credit window is tight, and Car 26's review is still the open unit. Dispatch when
that closes, with a stated cost line (1 Sonnet car + 1 Opus review).

## Next after that
#30 (conditions grouping/severity), #29 (IDLE is a state — owner-reserved design moment,
touches the CLOSED freshness union), #12 (health bar). Then the provenance spine as one
design moment: #25 multi-yard + #28 clickable provenance. Standing: #23, #24, #27.
From the Car 31 review: #35 (guard accepts `color:inherit`), #36 (`.track-title` residue,
owner decision, pairs with #29), #37 (register taxonomy pinning).

# RESUME HERE — checkpoint 2026-07-23, ~16:50 (rewritten mid-session)

**This checkpoint is written CONTINUOUSLY, not at session end.** A session can die
without warning (it did today: a credit limit killed session `67913b32` mid-work at
~16:21). A checkpoint that is only written at a graceful close does not exist when the
close is not graceful. Update this file the moment state changes — especially on dispatch.

## IN FLIGHT RIGHT NOW (dies with the session — re-dispatch from here)
- **Car 26 review** — Opus adversarial reviewer, detached worktree
  `<scratchpad>/review-car26` at frozen SHA `d0d9d77`. Reviewing Car 26's two commits
  (`969e856` Go mechanism, `d0d9d77` schema contract) against #26. If lost: re-dispatch
  from `docs/templates/car-brief.md`'s reviewer addendum; branch is `car/26-schema-drift`,
  base `38b67c8`. **Car 26 is NOT merged and must not merge without this verdict.**

## DONE AND PUSHED THIS SESSION
- **#31 CLOSED.** Car 31's fix merged to dev at `59bc98d`; APPROVE verdict landed verbatim
  at `artifacts/reviews/2026-07-23-car31-register-css-review-round1-APPROVE.md`
  (sha256 `aef0aeb1...`). Verify-Verdict: **50/50** bodies match. Conductor re-derived
  node **56/56** and Go **5/5** at the merge. CI green at `8f82464` (run 30044141768).
- dev tip `8a6cdb1`, pushed. GitNexus reindexed post-merge (1932 → **1961 symbols**);
  it asked for `gitnexus analyze --repair-fts` at some point — not chased, not blocking.

## THE LIVE DEFECT FOUND THIS SESSION (highest-value open thread)
**The producer hook silently skipped 2 of this session's 3 dispatches.** Only Car 26
(`a3bfb5c078bd8da96`) got a `dispatched` record; Car 31 and its reviewer got none, and
`git status` is clean so they were never written (not written-and-uncommitted, which is
the OTHER known failure mode — see `1182e09`). All day before this the producer was
regular: 20 dispatched records, ~10:14 through 15:00.

**Why this is structural, not cosmetic:** the harness detects a missing artifact by
finding a `dispatched` record with no matching `returned` one. A dispatch that never
writes a `dispatched` record is not overdue — it is INVISIBLE. The mechanism built to
make a killed session survivable has a blind spot exactly where the kill happens.
Root cause NOT yet established (hypothesis, unproven: git index contention with the
conductor's own concurrent heavy git operations right after dispatch). Do not assert a
cause without probing it.

## What landed earlier today (unchanged, still true)
The yard-board train (#1) is COMPLETE and FIRST-LIT: `board/` (Go server + vanilla-ESM
view), the fold, the producer budget stamp, four CI legs watched red AND green, README
quickstart verified from clean clones twice. Board rendered the real store and generated
its own backlog in its first minute. Story: `docs/retros/2026-07-23-board-train-retro.md`.
Also: #20 gate scoping, doc map + glossary, house vocabulary (NIRTS, HAVAGLANCE, FULL
SENTENCE FLOW, ask-don't-infer), mocks-as-direction, #22 effort/budget ruling.

## State
- Suites (baselines): Pester **247/247**, Go **5/5 packages**, node **50/50** (invoke from
  `board/web`, bare `node --test`), probe PASS, verdicts **49/49** hash-verified.
- Board server: `cd board && go run ./server` → http://127.0.0.1:4600.
- `main` is 40+ commits behind `dev`, deliberately (merge north star: wait for a state
  worth asserting). PR to main requires an owner ping FIRST.
- Housekeeping done: 18 open issues given their missing area label; 37 stale worktrees
  and 11 fully-merged `car/*` branches pruned.
- Go is NOT on PATH in either tool shell — full path `C:\Program Files\Go\bin\go.exe`.
  `sh` is absent from the pwsh tool's PATH (probe suites red there; known env gap).

## Next after the two dispatches land
#30 (conditions grouping/severity), #29 (IDLE is a state — owner-reserved design moment,
touches the CLOSED freshness union), #12 (health bar). Then the provenance spine as one
design moment: #25 multi-yard + #28 clickable provenance. Standing: #23, #24, #27.

## Standing reminders
- Model topology: conductor Fable, gates Opus, cars Sonnet ([[model-topology-owner-decision]]).
- Every push watched to a RECORDED terminal state (`scripts/Watch-CI.ps1`).
- Mocks are direction ([[mocks-are-direction-never-contract]]); ask-don't-infer on owner
  vocabulary ([[ask-dont-infer-owner-vocabulary]]); pwsh work through the PowerShell tool
  ([[bash-pwsh-boundary-discipline]]); PR needs an owner ping first
  ([[pr-requires-owner-ping-first]]).
- The owner runs UNANNOUNCED chaos-monkey drills ([[chaos-monkey-drills-are-unannounced]]).

input.command:
cd "C:/Users/Chris/git/starcar" && cat >> docs/friction-log.md << 'EOF'
| 07-23 | **`Land-Verdict.ps1` names the WRONG CAUSE when handed the wrong transcript.** The backfill path (used precisely because the producer hook had failed - see #32) wants the PARENT session transcript at `~/.claude/projects/<project>/<session-id>.jsonl`, because the `<result>` block lives there. Pointed at the obvious-looking per-task `.output` file (the subagent's own JSONL, whose path the harness hands you directly), it fails with *"No result block found for task id X. A dispatch that never completed has no verdict to land."* The dispatch had completed perfectly; only the input was wrong. A recovery tool that misattributes its own failure is expensive exactly when it is needed, and this one blamed the dispatch for an operator input error. Filed in #32. | One round trip, plus a moment of believing a completed dispatch had died | An error message that states a CAUSE rather than a CONDITION is a lying instrument; it should distinguish "no result block in this file" from "dispatch never completed" and name the path it expected |
| 07-23 | **The register taxonomy had SIX copies and ZERO mechanical pins** until the #31 fix accidentally added the first one - discovered by the Car 31 reviewer, who compared all six by hand and found them agreeing purely by luck and discipline. `compose.js` holds the severity ORDER as well as the set, and the schema enum cannot express ordering, so a reordering drift would have been silent in a way a set-comparison would not catch. The exemplar for fixing it (`sse-event-name.test.js`) was already in the same directory, unused as a pattern. Filed as #37. | Nil so far - all six agree today | A hand-maintained agreement that has never drifted looks identical to one that cannot drift; only a pin tells them apart |
EOF
tail -2 docs/friction-log.md | cut -c1-120

prior-art question is CLOSED with a real answer rather than an assumption: there is none
to port, because the sibling shop is a C# program using the Avalonia MCP and on-rig
testing, so its UI-verification pattern does not cross to a browser view. Asking cost one
sentence; the scar for not asking was five design rounds.

The owner also approved the three conductor refinements, which now BIND the eventual
brief: take the Playwright LIBRARY rather than @playwright/test so board/web keeps ONE
test runner; regenerate screenshots but NEVER pixel-diff them, because the windows and
ubuntu CI legs rasterise differently and a pixel assertion would cry wolf across legs;
and note that #32 is a BROKEN instrument while #33 is a missing one, which is a
sequencing argument the owner holds. The second refinement corrected this conductor's own
issue text, which had blurred regeneration into diffing.

Friction rows added:
- Land-Verdict.ps1 blames the DISPATCH ("a dispatch that never completed has no verdict

  instead of the parent session transcript. A recovery tool misattributing its own

  added the first. A hand-maintained agreement that has never drifted is
  indistinguishable from one that cannot drift; only a pin tells them apart.

content.text:
**#33 is approved and recorded — thank you for the prior-art answer, it closed a real question rather than leaving me to assume.** The sibling being C#/Avalonia with on-rig testing settles it: nothing to port. Your "amendment approved" means all three refinements now bind the eventual brief, and I've written them into the issue and the working checkpoint so whoever writes that brief inherits them rather than rediscovering them.

The refinement I care most about is the one that corrected my own issue text. I'd pitched Playwright as fixing screenshot drift, which reads as an invitation to pixel-diff — and pixel-diffing across our windows and ubuntu CI legs would fail constantly on font rasterisation alone, for reasons having nothing to do with the board being wrong. That's the crying-wolf shape we already have a scar for. Scope is now explicitly: regenerate the screenshot, assert on `getComputedStyle`, never on pixels.

**I have not dispatched #33 yet, deliberately.** You approved the scope; you haven't approved a spend, and you just told me credits were tight enough to kill a session. Cost discipline here says spend is agreed before dispatch, never discovered on the bill. So I'm holding at one unit at a time until Car 26's review closes, then dispatching with a stated cost line — one Sonnet car plus one Opus review. Say the word if you'd rather I run them concurrently.

**#31 is closed and merged.** The review came back APPROVE with zero Majors, and it did the job properly — re-derived the red byte-for-byte on a scratch copy, ran seven vacuity attacks on the guard (six killed it), and measured the fix rather than arguing it: 142 of 216 text-bearing elements move from red to paper, exactly the symptom the issue named. It also injected an `error` outcome and confirmed it *stays* red inside a now-calm chip, so the fix doesn't flatten severity. I re-ran both suites myself at the merge — node 56/56, Go 5/5 — and CI is green at a recorded terminal state.

Three things came out of that review as tickets rather than getting quietly folded in: **#35**, the guard accepts `color: inherit` and would pass while the defect is live (its own comment claims more than it checks); **#36**, `.track-title` and `.declared-not-observed` still wear the lane's register, which is a genuine visual decision that belongs with you alongside #29; **#37**, the register taxonomy lives in six places and only one edge is now pinned.

The reviewer also caught me a useful correction: Car 26 skipped GitNexus reindexing for the right reason but stated a false one ("CSS/test-only adds no symbols"). The reindex proved it wrong — 1932 symbols to 1961. Left unchallenged that reasoning would have licensed future cars to skip a mandated step on a bad premise, so it's now recorded and the reindex is done.

**#32 got sharper and it's worth your attention.** Both hooks fired for Car 26 and *neither* fired for the other two dispatches — so the producer failure selects the whole dispatch, not the hook or the moment. That materially weakens my original contention hypothesis, and I've said so on the issue rather than letting a tidy theory stand. The silver lining: `Land-Verdict.ps1` recovered the verdict verbatim from the session transcript, so nothing was lost, just not automatic. Verify-Verdict now reports 50/50 bodies matching their hashes.

Car 26's review is still running; that's the only thing in flight. `dev` is at `b675062`, pushed, with CI being watched to a recorded terminal state.

content.text:
Probing surfaced something that changes what you approved, and you should see it before I spend the budget.

**#33 is bigger than the "one spec file" I told you.** This repo has *no `package.json` anywhere* — it is deliberately dependency-free, with the JSON-schema validator vendored as checked-in source. Playwright can't be vendored; it needs browser binaries. So #33 introduces the repo's first `package.json`, first `node_modules`, and a CI browser-install step — and it invalidates three *declared* postures:

- `docs/setup.md`'s Node.js row says explicitly "test tooling, **not a browser dependency**" and "**no `npm install`**, no bundler"
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s node step comment says "**no framework, no build step, no browser**"
- `.gitignore` has no `node_modules` entry

None of that kills the case — the value argument stands and you ruled. But "no npm install" was a deliberate posture, not an accident, so changing it is your call to keep or reverse. I'm proceeding on your approval and making the doc updates mandatory in the brief; nothing reaches `main`, so it's reversible.

input.description: Car 33: Playwright computed-style guard
input.prompt:
You are Car 33 on the StarCar first-light iteration (repo: StarCar, a process-showcase repo; the yard board is a Go server + vanilla-ESM browser view under board/, no build step, no bundler). You implement issue #33, owner-approved with a binding amendment. Work ONLY in the worktree at C:/Users/Chris/AppData/Local/Temp/claude/C--Users-Chris-git-starcar/e92f5a0b-1b9e-4913-98b3-c319b4d5e90b/scratchpad/car33 on branch car/33-playwright. NEVER touch the shared checkout at C:/Users/Chris/git/starcar or any other worktree. Commit locally per task; NEVER push. NO NESTED DELEGATION.

Platform notes: Go is NOT on PATH - use `C:\Program Files\Go\bin\go.exe`. Node is on PATH. Node tests run from board/web with bare `node --test`. Pester via pwsh.

FIRST verify base: `git log -1 --format='%H'` must show b675062815fc1399edd3714588f5ed81f57a38dc and the branch must be car/33-playwright. If not, STOP and report.

WHY THIS EXISTS (the class, not the instance). Issue #31 was a defect where the board rendered every dispatch row, train chip and outcome in the needs-attention colour while the data resolved calm. It passed ALL 50 node tests, because board/web/test/minidom.js is a hand-rolled DOM shim with NO CSS engine - so the entire class of computed-style/cascade defects is structurally invisible to our suite. A human eye caught it at first light. The fix's own guard (board/web/test/register-css.test.js) is a REGEX OVER STYLESHEET TEXT: it pins the file's content, not a rendered pixel, and the Car 31 reviewer proved it passes while the defect is live if the declaration reads `color: inherit`. This car closes that gap with a real browser engine, so a cascade question is settled by MEASUREMENT rather than by reading - the probe doctrine's rule that behavioural claims are settled only by RUNNING.

THE OWNER'S BINDING AMENDMENT - these three constraints are RULED, not suggestions:
1. Use the PLAYWRIGHT LIBRARY, NOT `@playwright/test`. board/web runs bare `node --test` with no config file. A second test runner would mean two ways to write a test, two commands, two reporters, forever. Drive `chromium.launch()` from inside a normal `node --test` file. If you believe this is impossible, HONEST-STOP with the exact blocker rather than importing the test runner.
2. Screenshot REGENERATION only. NEVER pixel diffing. CI runs windows-latest AND ubuntu-latest legs; their font rasterisation differs, so a pixel-diff assertion would fail across legs for reasons unrelated to correctness - the crying-wolf failure mode this shop ranks worse than no instrument. Assert on COMPUTED VALUES via getComputedStyle. Do not add any image-comparison dependency.
3. Serve the REAL board from the REAL store. Not a mock, not a static fixture page. Drive the actual Go server.

SCOPE CORRECTION YOU MUST HONOR - this is bigger than "add a test file", and the conductor verified each of these at your base. OPEN EACH and confirm before relying on it; a mismatch is a brief defect and an HONEST-STOP:
- There is NO package.json anywhere in this repo (verified: recursive search, excluding node_modules, found none). You are introducing the FIRST one. Put it in board/web/ (the only place with node tests), never at the repo root, and keep it minimal: one devDependency, no scripts beyond what is needed, no lockfile-format surprises. COMMIT the lockfile (package-lock.json) - a reproducible toolchain is Law 7's stranger requirement.
- `.gitignore` (repo root) has NO node_modules entry. Add one.
- `docs/setup.md`'s Node.js row explicitly claims Node is "test tooling, not a browser dependency" and that the workflow involves "no `npm install`, no bundler". THIS COMMIT MAKES BOTH CLAIMS FALSE. Update that row honestly: state what changed, why (issue #33 and the #31 class it closes), and that the browser dependency is TEST-SIDE only - the shipped board/web/ runtime still has no build step and no npm dependency. Do NOT delete the historical reasoning; amend it the way this repo amends things, visibly.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s "Run board/web node --test suite" step carries a comment claiming "no framework, no build step, no browser". That becomes false. Update it in the SAME commit.
- That same CI step has a ZERO-TEST REFUSAL guard parsing the TAP reporter's `# tests N` trailer (because `node --test` exits 0 on a directory with no test files - a lying-instrument shape this repo rejects). PRESERVE that guard's behavior. If your change alters how the suite is invoked, the guard must still work; prove it.

YOUR ORDERS - red-first at every step (write the failing test, RUN it, confirm it fails FOR THE STATED REASON; a red failing for a different reason is a finding to report, never to paper over):

TASK 1 - PROBE FIRST, before designing anything. The desk cannot prove these; RUN them and quote observations:

# RESUME HERE — checkpoint 2026-07-23, ~16:50 (rewritten mid-session)

**This checkpoint is written CONTINUOUSLY, not at session end.** A session can die
without warning (it did today: a credit limit killed session `67913b32` mid-work at
~16:21). A checkpoint that is only written at a graceful close does not exist when the
close is not graceful. Update this file the moment state changes — especially on dispatch.

## IN FLIGHT RIGHT NOW (dies with the session — re-dispatch from here)
- **Car 26 review** — Opus adversarial reviewer, detached worktree
  `<scratchpad>/review-car26` at frozen SHA `d0d9d77`. Reviewing Car 26's two commits
  (`969e856` Go mechanism, `d0d9d77` schema contract) against #26. If lost: re-dispatch
  from `docs/templates/car-brief.md`'s reviewer addendum; branch is `car/26-schema-drift`,
  base `38b67c8`. **Car 26 is NOT merged and must not merge without this verdict.**

## DONE AND PUSHED THIS SESSION
- **#31 CLOSED.** Car 31's fix merged to dev at `59bc98d`; APPROVE verdict landed verbatim
  at `artifacts/reviews/2026-07-23-car31-register-css-review-round1-APPROVE.md`
  (sha256 `aef0aeb1...`). Verify-Verdict: **50/50** bodies match. Conductor re-derived
  node **56/56** and Go **5/5** at the merge. CI green at `8f82464` (run 30044141768).
- dev tip `8a6cdb1`, pushed. GitNexus reindexed post-merge (1932 → **1961 symbols**);
  it asked for `gitnexus analyze --repair-fts` at some point — not chased, not blocking.

## THE LIVE DEFECT FOUND THIS SESSION (highest-value open thread)
**The producer hook silently skipped 2 of this session's 3 dispatches.** Only Car 26
(`a3bfb5c078bd8da96`) got a `dispatched` record; Car 31 and its reviewer got none, and
`git status` is clean so they were never written (not written-and-uncommitted, which is
the OTHER known failure mode — see `1182e09`). All day before this the producer was
regular: 20 dispatched records, ~10:14 through 15:00.

**Why this is structural, not cosmetic:** the harness detects a missing artifact by
finding a `dispatched` record with no matching `returned` one. A dispatch that never
writes a `dispatched` record is not overdue — it is INVISIBLE. The mechanism built to
make a killed session survivable has a blind spot exactly where the kill happens.
Root cause NOT yet established (hypothesis, unproven: git index contention with the
conductor's own concurrent heavy git operations right after dispatch). Do not assert a
cause without probing it.

## What landed earlier today (unchanged, still true)
The yard-board train (#1) is COMPLETE and FIRST-LIT: `board/` (Go server + vanilla-ESM
view), the fold, the producer budget stamp, four CI legs watched red AND green, README
quickstart verified from clean clones twice. Board rendered the real store and generated
its own backlog in its first minute. Story: `docs/retros/2026-07-23-board-train-retro.md`.
Also: #20 gate scoping, doc map + glossary, house vocabulary (NIRTS, HAVAGLANCE, FULL
SENTENCE FLOW, ask-don't-infer), mocks-as-direction, #22 effort/budget ruling.

## State
- Suites (baselines): Pester **247/247**, Go **5/5 packages**, node **50/50** (invoke from
  `board/web`, bare `node --test`), probe PASS, verdicts **49/49** hash-verified.
- Board server: `cd board && go run ./server` → http://127.0.0.1:4600.
- `main` is 40+ commits behind `dev`, deliberately (merge north star: wait for a state
  worth asserting). PR to main requires an owner ping FIRST.
- Housekeeping done: 18 open issues given their missing area label; 37 stale worktrees
  and 11 fully-merged `car/*` branches pruned.
- Go is NOT on PATH in either tool shell — full path `C:\Program Files\Go\bin\go.exe`.
  `sh` is absent from the pwsh tool's PATH (probe suites red there; known env gap).

## APPROVED AND READY TO DISPATCH: #33 Playwright (owner ruling + amendment, 2026-07-23)
Owner approved in chat AND on the issue, for provenance. Prior-art question CLOSED: none
to port — the sibling is a C# program using the Avalonia MCP and on-rig testing, so its
UI-verification pattern does not cross to a browser view. **The owner's "amendment
approved" covers all three conductor refinements, which BIND the eventual brief:**
1. Playwright **library**, NOT `@playwright/test` — `board/web` runs bare `node --test`
   with no config; a second runner would mean two ways to write a test forever.
2. Screenshot **regeneration** only, **never pixel diffing** — CI has windows + ubuntu
   legs and their rasterisation differs, so a pixel assertion would cry wolf across legs
   (the 54-flags-50-false scar). Assert on `getComputedStyle`, not pixels.
3. Sequencing noted: #32 is a BROKEN existing instrument; #33 is a new one. A lying
   instrument poisons everything downstream, so #32 arguably goes first.
NIRTS scope: ONE spec inside the existing `node --test` suite that serves the real board
from the real store, reds first on the #31 defect (a nominal row inside a hot lane),
asserts computed colour per register, regenerates the screenshot. No second runner, no
pixel diffs, no page objects.
**Not dispatched yet — deliberately.** Cost discipline: one unit at a time while the

content.text:
Both trains are running now. Here's where things stand.

**The CI red was real, then correctly cleared — and the instruments earned their keep.** `Watch-CI.ps1` exited 10 (RED) on `b675062`: Windows failed, Ubuntu passed. Cause was the PowerShell Gallery flaking on `Install-Module Pester`, losing all three retries. This is the *same* substrate flake as founding-session run 29913822738 — the one that sat unnoticed for an hour while the conductor said "CI green" a dozen times. This time the retry guard labelled itself "INFRASTRUCTURE red, not a code red," the watcher's exit code made it impossible to miss, and I held off classifying it until a re-run of *identical code* passed both legs. Total triage: about four minutes, no false claim in between. That's the whole argument for landed instruments in one incident.

A second instrument fired cleanly too: when the producer hook auto-committed underneath me and moved HEAD, `Watch-CI` refused to watch an unpushed commit and exited **1 — could-not-observe**, deliberately distinct from red. It reported honest ignorance rather than inventing a conclusion. Both logged for the retro.

**I corrected my own #32 claim.** With five dispatches now sampled, the producer has recovered — 3 of 5 recorded, and the two failures are the earliest two, consecutive, after which it worked three times running across both models. My earlier "per-dispatch selector" framing was too sharp; it reads as a contiguous bad window (~16:38–16:42), which happens to coincide with my 37 worktree removals in the shared checkout. Suggestive, but it does *not* cleanly explain all the cases, so I've explicitly declined to promote it to a cause and recorded the walk-back on the issue rather than leaving the more confident-sounding version standing. The blocker is that there's no hook invocation log, so "never fired" and "fired and failed" are indistinguishable — instrument first.

**Train 2 is Car 33 (Playwright), dispatched with your budget.** The brief carries your amendment as binding — library not test-runner, regeneration not pixel-diffing, real server not mock — plus the scope correction I flagged, with all three doc updates mandatory in the same commit. Its first task is a probe, not a design: prove Playwright can be driven from inside `node --test` and that the real server can be launched and addressed, and honest-stop if either fails rather than quietly substituting a mock, which would defeat the ticket. The red that matters is reproducing #31 through a real browser, fault-injected twice — once by removing the declaration, once with `color: inherit`, the case that slips past the regex guard (#35). That second demonstration *is* the argument for the ticket.

Train 1, Car 26's review, is still running. Its Opus review will be owed for Car 33 too — that's the second half of train 2 and stays within your two-train cap. Board audit is clean: all 32 open issues visible on project 6.

`dev` is at `f358cad`, pushed, with CI being watched to a recorded terminal state. Nothing has gone near `main`.

input.message:
BINDING AMENDMENT - OWNER RULING, 2026-07-23, mid-dispatch. This SUPERSEDES the corresponding guidance in your brief. Everything else in your brief stands unchanged.

CONTEXT: your brief told you this commit "makes both claims false" in docs/setup.md's Node.js row (the "not a browser dependency" and "no npm install, no bundler" claims) and told you to update that row honestly. The owner has now ruled on WHAT honest means here, and it is narrower and more precise than "these claims are now false".

THE RULING, verbatim in substance: installing dependencies for Playwright was always going to happen; these are the kinds of installs that are LEGITIMATE and are TRIGGERED BY A GATE. We do not SHIP Playwright with the code - when the board is packaged, Playwright is not included. So the SPIRIT of the contract is met, if not the letter.

WHAT THIS CHANGES IN YOUR TASK:

1. Do NOT write the setup.md amendment as a retraction, a broken promise, or a posture reversal. It is not one. Write it as the EXISTING principle being applied to a new case.

2. The load-bearing distinction is SHIPPED RUNTIME versus TEST TOOLCHAIN, and docs/setup.md ALREADY draws exactly this line in the same Node.js row you are editing. Its own words: "D19's no-build-step rule binds the BROWSER (board/web's eventual runtime), never Node - Node is what RUNS the tests and probes against browser-shaped code before it ships." Open that row and read it before you write. Playwright sits on precisely the same side of that line as Node does. Your amendment should make the ALREADY-IMPLICIT rule EXPLICIT, so the next reader does not hit the same apparent contradiction the conductor did.

3. State the packaging fact as the reason, because it is the reason: Playwright is a devDependency that is never included when the board is packaged or deployed. The shipped board/web/ runtime still has no build step, no bundler, and no npm dependency - that claim remains TRUE and must be preserved in the row, not deleted. What changes is only that the TEST side now has an npm-installed dependency, gate-triggered.

4. Frame the install as GATE-TRIGGERED, which is this repo's normal, designed path for tooling - the same shape as every other row in setup.md's "Installs later - trigger-gated" table. The trigger that fired here is issue #33 and the #31 defect class it closes (a computed-style defect that passed all 50 node tests because minidom has no CSS engine). Name that trigger in the row. An install that arrives through its stated trigger is the system working, not an exception to it.

5. Apply the same framing to the ci.yml step comment. "no framework, no build step, no browser" describes what the SHIPPED view needs in order to run; it should be amended to keep that true while disclosing that the TEST leg now drives a real browser. Do not simply delete the sentence.

WHAT DOES NOT CHANGE: the amendment must still be honest and visible - do not quietly reword history so it reads as though nothing happened. The repo's rule is that documents are amended visibly, never rewritten in place to hide a change. A reader must be able to see that a browser dependency was added, when, why, and under what trigger. "Spirit met, not letter" is an argument for PRECISION in the amendment, never for silence about it.

If any of this contradicts what you have already written, revise it. If you have already committed a version, amend forward with a new commit rather than rewriting history, and disclose both in your report. If you believe this ruling is wrong or unimplementable as stated, say so explicitly in your report with evidence - disagreeing loudly is a success outcome here; disagreeing quietly is the failure.

CONSEQUENCE FOR THE WORK - Car 33's brief amended mid-flight:
1. The setup.md amendment is written as the EXISTING principle applied to a new case, NOT as a retraction or a broken promise.
2. The claim that the shipped board/web runtime has no build step, no bundler and no npm dependency remains TRUE and must be PRESERVED in the row, not deleted. Only the TEST side gains an npm dependency.

"Spirit met, not letter" is an argument for PRECISION in the amendment, never a licence for silence about it.

DOCTRINE WORTH KEEPING (candidate for setup.md's own framing, and arguably for CLAUDE.md if it recurs): a dependency that never ships cannot violate a no-dependency posture aimed at the shipped artifact. State which side of the shipped/toolchain line a new dependency falls on, and the apparent contradiction dissolves before it costs anyone a review round.

content.text:
Understood, and your framing is better than mine — I was reading the letter and missing that the document already contains the principle.

**The ruling is recorded and delivered.** Two places, because it needs to bind in two different ways:

- **To Car 33, as a binding mid-flight amendment.** It's editing `docs/setup.md` and `ci.yml` right now, and my original brief told it those claims "become false" and to update them accordingly. That would have produced a retraction-flavoured edit — the wrong shape. The amendment supersedes that: write it as the *existing* principle applied to a new case, preserve the still-true claim that the shipped `board/web` runtime has no build step, bundler, or npm dependency, and name the trigger (#33, closing the #31 class) so the install reads as the gate working rather than an exception to it.

- **On #33, as provenance**, with the reasoning that makes it durable rather than a one-off judgement call.

The part that makes this a real ruling and not a rationalisation is that `setup.md` already draws the exact line, in the very row I flagged: *"D19's no-build-step rule binds the BROWSER (`board/web`'s eventual runtime), never Node — Node is what RUNS the tests and probes against browser-shaped code before it ships."* Playwright sits on the same side of that line as Node does. The principle was there; it just wasn't explicit enough for a cold reader to carry to the next case — which is precisely how I stumbled into calling it a posture reversal. So the amendment's job is to make the implicit rule explicit, and the general form is worth keeping: **a dependency that never ships cannot violate a no-dependency posture aimed at the shipped artifact.** State which side of the shipped/toolchain line a new dependency falls on, and the apparent contradiction dissolves before it costs a review round.

One thing I held firm on in the amendment, and want to flag since it cuts against the convenient reading: "spirit met, not letter" is an argument for *precision* in how the doc is amended, never a licence for silence about it. The car is still required to make the change visible — that a browser dependency was added, when, why, under what trigger — rather than quietly rewording the row so it reads as though nothing happened. Documents get amended visibly here; that constraint is untouched by the ruling.

I also told the car that if it thinks the ruling is wrong or unimplementable as stated, it must say so loudly in its report with evidence. Disagreeing quietly is the failure mode, including with the owner.

Both trains still running: Car 26's review, and Car 33. `dev` is at `fc73dc1` with the two friction rows committed, CI being watched to a recorded terminal state.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: this is a good lesson of reality not always meeting spec. There are gonna be times when this happens and it's the right move to kick it up to the human in the loop for final adjudication, even if it means sharpening the definition or loosening it as needed, but it's a case by case basis, never assumed, never inferred, never projected from your end.
